reasoning: I have observed initial values x0=0, v=5, t=2, and resulting x=10. I need to find the relationship. I will try increasing v to see if x changes proportionally or follows a different pattern.

This suggests a linear relationship where x is proportional to v, specifically x = k*v. With t constant, the relationship might be x = v * t / 2 or simply x = v * t if the constant is 1. Let's test the hypothesis x = v * t by changing t. If I decrease t to 1, x should become 5 (if x=v*t) or 2.5 (if x=v*t/2). I will decrease t to test the exact coefficient.

I have collected three data points: (v=5, t=2, x=10), (v=10, t=2, x=20), and (v=10, t=1, x=10). Analyzing these, when v is constant at 10, x is proportional to t (10*2=20, 10*1=10). When t is constant at 2, x is proportional to v (5*2=10, 10*2=20). This strongly suggests the equation is x = v * t. I will test this hypothesis by changing v while keeping t=1. If the equation holds, changing v to 5 should result in x=5.

I have collected four data points: (v=5, t=2, x=10), (v=10, t=2, x=20), (v=10, t=1, x=10), and (v=5, t=1, x=5). In all cases, x equals the product of v and t (5*2=10, 10*2=20, 10*1=10, 5*1=5). The relationship x = v * t is consistent across all observations. I will perform one final check by increasing v to 15 with t=1 to see if x becomes 15, confirming the linear proportionality before finishing.
